record.field:
The Grocery Shop

Setting up a Point of Sale (POS) system for a grocery store which is essential for efficiently managing various product categories, handling transactions, and providing excellent customer service.Grocery stores are businesses that carry a large selection of products like Food Grains, oil & Clarified Butter, Dry Fruits, Chocolates , snacks & Packaged Food with multiple variants etc…Here's a step-by-step guide to setting up a POS system for a grocery store, also known as a Grocery store, supermarket, or food store:


In the retail store, we set up the POS module for B2C customers and sale for B2B customers. They use the Sales, Purchase, Inventory, Accounting, Contact, Employee modules.


Flow 1 : Purchase Flow. Flow 2: Point of sale - Regular flow with Buy one get one free product Flow 3: Point of sale - Customer Account  Flow 4: Point of Sale - Ship Later & Global discount @10%  Flow 5: Sale Flow – B2B Customer

Flow 1 : Purchase Flow.


  • Create an RFQ for "Grocery outlet"
  • Select products (e.g Aashirvaad Chakki Atta)
  • Confirm the RFQ
  • Validate the reception for all quantities at once.


Flow 2: Point of sale - Regular flow with Buy one get one free product


For the Small Products, orders & payments are done at the shop itself, and the customer pickups the products      directly.


 To do that, start with a POS order:


  •  Create a POS order with the required product (i.e. ‘Rozana super Basmati Rice)and (Maxfresh with cooling crystals     Toothpaste — 600g – Pack of 4)
  •  Click on the “Reward” Button then after automatic Free product add “‘Toothbrush Ultra soft[Pack of 4].
  •  Then pay the order.


Flow 3: Point of sale - Customer Account 


The concept is simple from the POS screen itself, When a customer purchases a product on credit, it means they take the product without making an immediate payment. Instead, they owe a certain amount to the shop. The customer returns to the shop at a later date to pay the remaining amount they owe for the product, settling the debt.


To do that, start with a POS order:


  • Create a POS order with the required product (i.e.Maggie Mega Pack” – 70g - Pack of 4 , sunfeast Dark Fantasy choco     fills Cream – 600g - Pack of 4)
  • Then, we can add a “customer note” i.e put in some money as the credit balance.
  • Validate your order.

Flow 4: Point of Sale - Ship Later & Global discount @10% 


For large or Bulk products, the order & payment is done at the shop, but the delivery is sometimes scheduled later.


To do that, start with a POS order:


  • Create a POS order with heavy products (i.e. Almonds(American), Cadbury Oreo Biscuit Vanilla Flavour Cream)
  • ​Give the Global Discount @ 10%
  • Set the customer to define the shipping address
  • As it's set an invoice and mark "Ship Later"
  • Then pay the order

Flow 5: Sale Flow – B2B Customer


Customers can book the order, or ask for a quote, on the phone at the shop, but the customer picks up and pays for the products at the store when they are available.


To do that, start with a Quotation order:


  • Create a quotation and send it by email 
  • Optionally: the customer might confirm the order, but it's not necessary
  • When the customer goes to the store, from the POS Order, use the button "Quotation / Order" to load the related         sale order
  • Settle the order from the POS screen to record the payment and give products to the customer.
